fields.name: Mammalia
fields.description: Warm-blooded vertebrates characterised by hair and live birth.

fields.name: Amphibia
fields.description: Cold-blooded vertebrates that can live in water and on land.

fields.known_range_description: Patchy distribution across northern Australia, including Arnhem Land escarpment and some NT coastal regions.
fields.description: Australia's most endangered native carnivore. Nocturnal and solitary, with distinctive spotted coat. Produces a range of vocalisations including hisses and churring calls.

fields.known_range_description: Rocky gorge systems and caves across the NT, including Nitmiluk and areas near Alice Springs.
fields.description: Australia's only carnivorous bat. Uses echolocation clicks as well as social vocalisations. Roosts in colonies in caves and mine shafts.

fields.known_range_description: Arid and semi-arid regions of central and western NT. Australia's largest lizard.
fields.description: Australia's largest lizard, reaching up to 2.5m. Produces a deep hissing exhalation when threatened. Found in rocky outcrops and desert plains.

fields.known_range_description: Widespread across northern and eastern Australia. Common in the Top End NT near water sources and vegetation.
fields.description: One of Australia's most recognisable frogs. Produces a loud, slow 'croak' call, particularly active during the wet season. Often found near human dwellings.

fields.known_range_description: Widespread across arid and semi-arid Australia, including central NT. Found near water in dry inland areas.
fields.description: Australia's smallest dove. Produces a soft, mournful cooing call. Often seen in pairs foraging on the ground.

fields.known_range_description: Widespread across mainland Australia including most of the NT. Absent from dense rainforest and very arid central regions.
fields.description: Australia's largest bird. Produces a deep booming drum call, made by females. Males produce a grunting sound. Notable for the Beetaloo region population.

fields.known_range_description: Restricted to the Pilbara (WA) and Top End NT, particularly the Beetaloo Sub-basin and surrounding cave systems. Listed as of critical concern in the Beetaloo region.
fields.description: A small, bright-orange bat of high conservation significance in the Beetaloo Sub-basin. Highly sensitive to roost disturbance. Produces high-frequency echolocation calls.

fields.known_range_description: Highly restricted range in central-southern NT and northern South Australia. Found in dense lignum and samphire shrublands.
fields.description: A cryptic, ground-dwelling bird with a distinctive rattling call. One of Australia's least-studied birds. Extremely sensitive to habitat change.
